title: caladium bicolor var. brongniartii
intro: Caladium bicolor var. brongniartii, also known as Elephant's Ear, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Arum family. It is widely cultivated for its striking, heart-shaped leaves featuring white and green variegation patterns, and is prized for its ornamental value in gardens and indoor spaces. The plant's leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves can be prepared for medicinal use through infusion or decoction, and are sometimes used in herbal teas, salves, and tinctures to promote overall health and well-being.
